title: The Day the Crayons Quit
mainIdea: Different people have different needs, and listening to them can help solve problems.

pages.text: Duncan wanted to color. When he opened his crayon box, he found letters instead of crayons.

question.text: Who is the main character in the story?

pages.text: The crayons said they were tired and wanted changes.

question.text: What did Duncan want to do?

pages.text: Beige was tired of being ignored. Black wanted to color more than outlines.

question.text: What problem did the crayons have?
options: They were unhappy with their jobs

pages.text: Blue was tired from coloring water. Orange and Yellow argued about who was the true color of the sun.

question.text: What did Duncan learn from the letters?
options: The crayons wanted to leave school

pages.text: Duncan needed to find a way to make all the crayons happy so they would color again.

options: Duncan tried to help the crayons so they could color again

elements.correct: The crayons quit because they were unhappy
He tried to make them happy so they could color again
